annotations.label: Tangent line to a plane curve

anchor.snippet: a straight line is tangent to the curve

annotations.note: Mathlib has `tangentConeAt` (tangent directions to a set at a point) and `HasDerivAt`/`HasFDerivAt`, but not a named `tangentLine` construct for plane curves.

annotations.label: Point of tangency

anchor.snippet: The point where the tangent line and the curve meet or intersect is called the point of tangency

annotations.note: The notion is captured for spheres via `IsTangentAt` (point at which an affine subspace touches a sphere), but no general `pointOfTangency` exists.

annotations.label: Tangent line approximation

anchor.snippet: can also be thought of as a tangent line approximation

annotations.note: Mathlib's `HasFDerivAt`/`HasDerivAt` encode the first-order linear approximation, but no specific 'tangent line approximation' lemma.

annotations.label: Tangent plane to a surface (informal)

anchor.snippet: the tangent plane to a surface at a given point is the plane that

annotations.note: Tangent planes for embedded surfaces are not formalized; the manifold `TangentSpace` is the closest analogue.

annotations.label: Leibniz tangent (limit of secants)

anchor.snippet: the tangent to a curve at a point P on the curve is the limit of the line passing through two points of the curve

annotations.note: For graphs of functions, `hasDerivAt_iff_tendsto_slope` formalizes the limit-of-secants characterization.

annotations.label: Tangent as limit of secant lines

anchor.section: Tangent line to a plane curve
anchor.snippet: The tangent at A is the limit when point B approximates or tends to A

annotations.note: Captured for function graphs by `hasDerivAt_iff_tendsto_slope`; no general curve-theoretic version.

annotations.label: Inflection point

anchor.section: Tangent line to a plane curve
anchor.snippet: A point where the tangent (at this point) crosses the curve is called an inflection point

annotations.note: No `Inflection`/`inflectionPoint` declaration was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inflection points of common curves

anchor.section: Tangent line to a plane curve
anchor.snippet: Circles , parabolas , hyperbolas and ellipses do not have any inflection point

annotations.note: No formalization of inflection-point classification for conics in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supporting line

anchor.section: Tangent line to a plane curve
anchor.snippet: In convex geometry , such lines are called supporting lines

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsExposed` (exposed faces by supporting hyperplanes) but no named 'supporting line'.

annotations.label: Slope of secant via difference quotient

anchor.snippet: The slope of the secant line passing through p and q is equal to the difference quotient

annotations.note: `slope f a b := (b - a)⁻¹ • (f b -ᵥ f a)` is exactly the secant difference quotient.

annotations.label: Slope of tangent as limit of difference quotients

anchor.snippet: This leads to the definition of the slope of the tangent line to the graph as the limit of the difference quotients

annotations.note: `hasDerivAt_iff_tendsto_slope` states `HasDerivAt f f' x ↔ Tendsto (slope f x) (𝓝[≠] x) (𝓝 f')`.

annotations.label: Vertical tangent: y = x^(1/3)

anchor.snippet: This curve has a tangent line at the origin that is vertical

annotations.note: The vertical-tangent example for x^(1/3) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cusp: y = x^(2/3)

anchor.snippet: this graph has a cusp at the origin

annotations.note: No notion of `Cusp` and no formalization of this specific example was found.

annotations.label: Corner: y = |x|

anchor.snippet: Having two different (but finite) slopes is called a corner

annotations.note: Non-differentiability of `|x|` at 0 isn't packaged as a 'corner' example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discontinuity implies non-differentiability

anchor.snippet: since differentiability implies continuity, the contrapositive states discontinuity implies non-differentiability

annotations.note: `DifferentiableAt.continuousAt` gives differentiability ⇒ continuity; the contrapositive is immediate.

annotations.label: Equation of tangent line via point-slope

anchor.snippet: so by the point–slope formula the equation of the tangent line at

annotations.note: The explicit `y = f(a) + f'(a)(x - a)` tangent-line equation isn't a named lemma.

annotations.label: Tangent via polynomial division

anchor.snippet: the tangent line's equation can also be found

annotations.note: No formalization of computing tangent lines to polynomial graphs by Euclidean division was found.

annotations.label: Tangent via implicit differentiation

anchor.snippet: the value of the slope can be found by implicit differentiation

annotations.note: Mathlib's implicit function theorem provides the underlying machinery but doesn't state the tangent-slope formula directly.

annotations.label: Singular point

anchor.snippet: the tangent line is not defined and the point ( X , Y ) is said to be singular

annotations.note: No `singularPoint`/`IsSingular` notion for plane curves was found.

annotations.label: Tangent line in homogeneous coordinates

anchor.snippet: It follows that the homogeneous equation of the tangent line is

annotations.note: No tangent-in-projective-coordinates lemma was found.

annotations.label: Tangent to a parametric curve

anchor.snippet: If the curve is given parametrically by

annotations.note: No tangent-line construction specifically for parametric curves was found.

annotations.label: Normal line to a curve

anchor.snippet: The line perpendicular to the tangent line to a curve at the point of tangency is called the normal line to the curve at that point

annotations.note: No `normalLine` declaration exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equation of the normal line

anchor.snippet: it follows that the equation of the normal line at

annotations.note: No formalization of an explicit normal-line equation was found.

annotations.label: Angle between curves

anchor.snippet: The angle between two curves at a point where they intersect is defined as the angle between their tangent lines at that point

annotations.note: No `angleBetweenCurves` definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent and orthogonal curves

anchor.snippet: two curves are said to be tangent at a point if they have the same tangent at a point, and orthogonal if their tangent lines are orthogonal

annotations.note: No notion of tangent/orthogonal curves was found.

annotations.label: Tangents at a singular point via lowest-degree terms

anchor.snippet: the equations of these lines can be found for algebraic curves by factoring the equation formed by eliminating all but the lowest degree terms

annotations.note: Tangent cones of algebraic curves via initial forms are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangents to the limaçon trisectrix at the origin

anchor.snippet: the equation of the limaçon trisectrix shown to the right is

annotations.note: The limaçon trisectrix is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left and right tangents

anchor.snippet: the tangents at that point with those slopes are called the left and right tangents

annotations.note: No left/right tangent line definitions; only one-sided derivatives via `HasDerivWithinAt`.

annotations.label: Coinciding left/right tangents for y = x^(2/3)

anchor.snippet: both the left and right tangent lines have equation x = 0

annotations.note: This specific example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent vector

anchor.section: Tangent line to a space curve
anchor.snippet: a tangent vector is a vector that is tangent to a curve or surface at a given point

annotations.note: Tangent vectors live in `TangentSpace I x`, defined for a point of a manifold.

annotations.label: Tangent vector as linear derivation on germs

anchor.section: Tangent line to a space curve
anchor.snippet: a tangent vector at the point

annotations.note: `PointDerivation I x` is the space of derivations on smooth functions at `x`, modelling tangent vectors as derivations.

annotations.label: Tangent circles

anchor.snippet: Two distinct circles lying in the same plane are said to be tangent to each other if they meet at exactly one point

annotations.note: Mathlib defines tangency between spheres (`IsExtTangent`, `IsIntTangent`), which specialises to tangent circles in dimension 2.

annotations.label: Externally and internally tangent circles

anchor.snippet: The two circles are called externally tangent if the distance between their centres is equal to the sum of their radii

annotations.note: `isExtTangent_iff_dist_center` and `isIntTangent_iff_dist_center` characterise external/internal tangency by `dist = r₁ ± r₂`.

annotations.label: Tangent plane to a surface

anchor.snippet: The tangent plane to a surface at a given point p is defined in an analogous way to the tangent line in the case of curves

annotations.note: The manifold `TangentSpace` covers this in abstract form, but no construction for embedded surfaces in ℝ³ is given.

annotations.label: Equation of the tangent plane

anchor.snippet: the equation of the tangent plane at point

annotations.note: No explicit tangent-plane equation for graphs/zero-sets was found.

annotations.label: Tangent space of a manifold

anchor.snippet: there is a k -dimensional tangent space at each point of a k -dimensional manifold

annotations.note: `TangentSpace I x` defines the tangent space at a point of a manifold modelled on `I`.
